title: Testing, Release Gates, and Memory Incident Defense · Independent C Trial
summary: An independent maintainer ports the production memory-safety defense dossier to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of production memory-safety defense dossier with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.
Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.
Pin source, dependencies, compiler, flags, generated files, symbols, SBOM, provenance, and artifact identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Memory Test Layers
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 4323. Produce bounded C evidence to decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Memory Test Layers.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, abi, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Memory Test Layers, keep Memory Test Layers connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Memory Incident Response
steps.instruction:
For this lab demonstration of cmem-memory-incident-response, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-memory-incident-response-57td5a */" for changed case 9443. It must decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
